sections.paragraph:
fut. יִשְׁכֹּן.-

(1) to let oneself down, to settle down, e.g. the pillar of fire and cloud, Num 9:17, 22 10:12 Exo 24:16.

(2) to lie down, especially to take rest, used of the lion, Deu 33:20 of a nation lying in tents, encamped, Num 24:2 of clouds overshadowing any day, followed by עַל Job 3:5. Hence to rest, Jud. 5:17 Pro 7:11, בְּבֵיתָהּ לֹא יִשְׁכְּנוּ רַגְלֶיהָ “her feet rest not in her house.”

(3) to dwell, to abide (Arab. سكن id.), followed by בְּ of place, Gen 9:27 14:13 26:2 Jud. 8:11 followed by an acc. of place, Isa 33:16 Psa 68:7. Of frequent occurrence is the phrase שָׁכַן אֶרֶץ to dwell in, or inhabit the land, to possess it quietly, Pro 2:21 10:30 Psa 37:29 and without אֶרֶץ Ps. 102:29 (compare a similar ellipsis, Isa 57:15, “God, שֹׁכֵן עַד who inhabits (the heavens) for ever”); 2Sa 7:10, וְשָׁכַן תַּחְתָּיו “and (the people) shall inhabit in their own place;” Deu 33:16, שֹׁכְנִי סְנֶה “the dweller in the bush,” i.e. Jehovah, compare Exo 3:2. With a dative pleon. Psa 120:6, שָֽׁכְנָה לָהּ “to dwell for oneself.”-Part. pass. שָׁכוּן act. dwelling, like the French logé, Jud. 8:11 .-Once metaph. to dwell in any thing is used for to be familiar with it (see סָכַן and Arab. سكن to be familiar), Pro 8:12, “I, wisdom, עָרְמָה שָׁכַנְתִּי dwell in prudence,” am altogether acquainted with her.

(4) pass. to be inhabited, as a place, i.q. יָשַׁב No. 4, Jer 50:39 33:16 46:26 Isa 13:20 used of a tent which is pitched, Jos 22:19.

Piel, to cause to dwell, Jer 7:7 Num 14:30. Jehovah is sometimes said שִׁכֵּן שְׁמֹו i.e. to fix his abode any where [cause his name to dwell], Deu 12:11 14:23 16:6 , 11 Deu 16:11 26:2 , i.q. שׂוּם שְׁמֹו under שׂוּם No. 1, f. Hence may be explained Psa 78:60, אֹהֶל שִׁכֵֹּו בָּאָדָם “the tent (in which) he caused (his name) to dwell amongst men,” unless it be preferred “the tent which he placed amongst men,” compare Jos 18:1 22:19 . Also from this phrase is taken the Talmudic שְׁכִינָה “presence of God.”

Hiphil, to cause any one to dwell, Gen 3:24 Job 11:14 to place a tent, Jos 18:1 (compare Kal, Jos 22:19); Psa 7:6, כְּבֹודִי לֶעָפָר יַשְׁכֵּן “let him make me (my honour) to dwell in the dust,” prostrate me on the dust.

Derivatives, שָׁכֵן-שְׁכַנְיָהוּ, מִשְׁכָּן.
